yafs.placement¶

Placement (allocation) algorithms for YAFS.

Each placement algorithm must provide two core methods:

  • initial_allocation: invoked at the start of the simulation.

  • run: invoked during the simulation according to the configured activation distribution.

class yafs.placement.ClusterPlacement(name, activation_dist=None, logger=None)[source]¶

Place application services in a cluster of nodes.

This implementation locates the services of the application in the “cluster” regardless of where the sources or sinks are located.

It only runs once, in the initialization.

get_next_activation()¶

Return the next simulation time at which this placement should run.

initial_allocation(sim, app_name)[source]¶

Perform the initial allocation of modules in the topology.

Args:
sim (yafs.core.Sim):

Simulation object.

app_name (str):

Application identifier.

Attention

override required

run(sim)¶

(Optionally) change the assignment of the modules during simulation.

Args:

sim (yafs.core.Sim): Simulation object.

scaleService(scale)¶

Configure how many replicas of specific modules should be deployed.

scale is usually a dictionary where keys are module names and values are the desired number of replicas.

class yafs.placement.EdgePlacement(name, activation_dist=None, logger=None)[source]¶

Place application services across cluster, edge (proxy), and mobile nodes.

It only runs once, in the initialization.

get_next_activation()¶

Return the next simulation time at which this placement should run.

initial_allocation(sim, app_name)[source]¶

Perform the initial allocation of modules in the topology.

Args:
sim (yafs.core.Sim):

Simulation object.

app_name (str):

Application identifier.

Attention

override required

run(sim)¶

(Optionally) change the assignment of the modules during simulation.

Args:

sim (yafs.core.Sim): Simulation object.

scaleService(scale)¶

Configure how many replicas of specific modules should be deployed.

scale is usually a dictionary where keys are module names and values are the desired number of replicas.

class yafs.placement.JSONPlacement(json, **kwargs)[source]¶
get_next_activation()¶

Return the next simulation time at which this placement should run.

initial_allocation(sim, app_name)[source]¶

Allocate modules according to a JSON specification.

The JSON structure is expected to contain an initialAllocation list with elements of the form:

{
    "app": "<app_name>",
    "module_name": "<module>",
    "id_resource": <node_id>
}
run(sim)¶

(Optionally) change the assignment of the modules during simulation.

Args:

sim (yafs.core.Sim): Simulation object.

scaleService(scale)¶

Configure how many replicas of specific modules should be deployed.

scale is usually a dictionary where keys are module names and values are the desired number of replicas.

class yafs.placement.JSONPlacementOnCloud(json, idCloud, **kwargs)[source]¶
get_next_activation()¶

Return the next simulation time at which this placement should run.

initial_allocation(sim, app_name)[source]¶

Allocate all modules of an application to a single cloud node.

The JSON structure is similar to JSONPlacement, but id_resource is ignored and idCloud is used instead.

run(sim)¶

(Optionally) change the assignment of the modules during simulation.

Args:

sim (yafs.core.Sim): Simulation object.

scaleService(scale)¶

Configure how many replicas of specific modules should be deployed.

scale is usually a dictionary where keys are module names and values are the desired number of replicas.

class yafs.placement.NoPlacementOfModules(name, activation_dist=None, logger=None)[source]¶

Placement strategy that does not allocate any modules.

Useful for scenarios where modules are manually deployed elsewhere, or placement is completely external to this mechanism.

get_next_activation()¶

Return the next simulation time at which this placement should run.

initial_allocation(sim, app_name)[source]¶

Perform the initial allocation of modules in the topology.

Args:
sim (yafs.core.Sim):

Simulation object.

app_name (str):

Application identifier.

Attention

override required

run(sim)¶

(Optionally) change the assignment of the modules during simulation.

Args:

sim (yafs.core.Sim): Simulation object.

scaleService(scale)¶

Configure how many replicas of specific modules should be deployed.

scale is usually a dictionary where keys are module names and values are the desired number of replicas.

class yafs.placement.Placement(name, activation_dist=None, logger=None)[source]¶

Base class for placement (allocation) algorithms.

A placement algorithm controls where to locate service modules and their replicas on the different nodes of the topology, according to load criteria or other objectives.

Note

A class interface

Args:

name (str): Associated name for the placement policy.

activation_dist (Distribution): Distribution that determines when

run() is invoked during simulation time.

Kwargs:

param (dict): Parameters for activation_dist.

get_next_activation()[source]¶

Return the next simulation time at which this placement should run.

initial_allocation(sim, app_name)[source]¶

Perform the initial allocation of modules in the topology.

Args:
sim (yafs.core.Sim):

Simulation object.

app_name (str):

Application identifier.

Attention

override required

run(sim)[source]¶

(Optionally) change the assignment of the modules during simulation.

Args:

sim (yafs.core.Sim): Simulation object.

scaleService(scale)[source]¶

Configure how many replicas of specific modules should be deployed.

scale is usually a dictionary where keys are module names and values are the desired number of replicas.
